description: (For 8360 Switch series only) Symptom: Wireless clients were unable to connect to the network when they roam from a VSX primary switch to a VSX secondary switch and did not get access to the network until one iteration of MAC ageout configuration. Scenario: This issue is observed when wireless clients move from an AP in VSX primary switch to another AP in VSX secondary switch and when both the APs connected to the switch ports operate in device mode. description: Symptom: IGMP/MLD control packets were not processed and the querier information was not learnt. Scenario: This issue is observed in a VXLAN setup without IGMP/MLD configurations where multicast control packets arrive continuously through the VLAN which does not have IGMP/MLD configuration. Workaround: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on all the VLANs where multicast clients send IGMP/MLD control packets.

description: (For 8360 Switch series only) Symptom: A PTP-BC might insert the wrote Steps Removed value into the next announce message it sends to the downstream clocks if it sees another announce message from another PTP-BC on its Clock Sink interface. Scenario: This issue is observed when some downstream clocks restart their PTP BMCA process when they see a change in the Steps Removed field within the PTP-BC's announce messages. Workaround: Configure an ACL that restricts PTP announce messages to the potential PTP-GMs, and apply it on the Clock Sink port.

Scenario: This issue is observed when the switch loses its synchronization with Grand-Source Clock and when the system is running for more than a day with multiple PTP clients connected directly to the switch. Workaround: Restart the ptpd daemon and reboot the switch.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

description: Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1

description: Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. description: Symptom: Clients fail to connect to the network post successful authentication. Scenario: This issue is observed when clients with a downloadable role try to onboard with temporary network issues. Workaround: Log off all the clients with the DUR in failed state using the port-access log-off client role <role-name> command to initiate a retry to download the role.

description: (For 8360 Switch series only) Symptom: A PTP-BC might insert the wrote Steps Removed value into the next announce message it sends to the downstream clocks if it sees another announce message from another PTP-BC on its Clock Sink interface. Scenario: This issue is observed when some downstream clocks restart their PTP BMCA process when they see a change in the Steps Removed field within the PTP-BC's announce messages. Workaround: Configure an ACL that restricts PTP announce messages to the potential PTP-GMs, and apply it on the Clock Sink port.

description: (For 8360 Switch series only) Symptom: The PTP daemon becomes unresponsive and the Offset From Clock-Source output from the show ptp command is stuck. Scenario: This issue is observed when the switch loses its synchronization with Grand-Source Clock and when the system is running for more than a day with multiple PTP clients connected directly to the switch. Workaround: Restart the ptpd daemon and reboot the switch.
